the assessorshallperform such otherdutiesas may be
prescribedby ordinance.
§ 84. He shallbe paid such compensation as may be
prescribedby ordinance,which shallnotbe changed
during his term of office.
Marshal .
§ 85. There shallbe electedat the regularelection
in November , one thousand eighthundred and ninety-
three,and every four years thereafter,by thequalified
voters of the city,a citymarshal,who shallhold his
officefour years,and untilhis successoriselectedand
qualified.
§ 86. No person shall be eligibleto the officeof
marshal who , at the time of his election,has not at-
tained the age of twenty-four years,and who has not
actuallyresidedand been a qualifiedvoter in said
city two years next preceding hiselection.
§ 87. The citymarshal shallnot be eligibleforthe
succeeding term, nor shallhe serve as deputy marshal
for the succeeding term .
§ 88. He shallwait upon the policecourt of the city
as the sheriffisrequiredto wait upon the circuitcourt
of his county . He shallbe a peace officerof the city
and the Commonwealth , and the chief ministerial
officerof the policecourt of the city.
§ 89. It shallbe the duty of the marshal to attend
all sessionsof the police court and of the common
council , and to preserve order, and to obey the lawful
mandates of the court,counciland mayor .
§ 90. He shall have the power to execute and return
all civil, penal and criminalprocess issuingfrom any
of the courts,or by any officerof the Commonwealth ,
and his jurisdictionshall be co-extensive with his
county ,but he shallnot be requiredto execute any
process from any court except the policecourt of his
city .
§ 91. He shallexecute allprocess or notice issued
by the cityclerk, by the mayor ,or by any board or

1090 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
committee authorized to issue same , or the clerk
thereof .
§ 92. He shallperform such other duties as may be
prescribedhereinor by ordinance.
§ 93. He shallkeep an officein the cityhall, or some
other convenientplace to be designated by the coun-
cil,and provided at the expense of the city, and he
or one of hisdeputies shallbe in the officeduring all
businesshours of the day .
§ 94. He may ,by writing, signedby him and ap-
provedby thecounciland enteredupon his journal,
appoint as many deputies to assisthim in the dis-
charge of his duties as he may deem necessary, who
shall have all the powers of the marshal, and for
whose actsand omissionsthe marshal shallbe respon-
sible on his officialbond .
§95.He shallenterupon the dischargeof theduties
of his officeon the firstMonday in December next
afterhis election, but not until he shall have executed
bond with sufficientsecurityto be approved by the
council .
§ 96. The marshal shallreceivesuch compensation
inthe way of salary, commissions and feesas shallbe
prescribedherein,or by ordinance,which shallnot be
changedduringhisterm of office: Provided,however,
For servicesother than for the city, he shall receive
the same fees and commissions allowed by law to
sheriffsand constables for similar service.
§ 97. For serviceof process for the city, or in the
name of the city,he shall receivethe same fees and
commissions allowed by law to sheriffsand constables
for similarservice, provided same shall be collected
from the defendant ; but in no case shall the city be
liablefor fees or commissions, unless same shall be
collectedand receivedby the cityin money .
§ 98. He shallnot engage in any businessor receive
any processthat interferes, or is in any way incom-
patible, with his duties to the city. He shall settle
his accounts with the city monthly , in the manner

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1091
prescribedby ordinance,thesame tobe examined and
certifiedby the auditorand mayor ,and approvedby
the council: Provided , The common councilmay ,by
ordinance, abolish or restore the officeof marshal,
and when so abolished the duties herein imposed on
the marshal shallbe performed by such officeras the
common councilshalldesignateor appoint.
Prosecuting Attorney .
•§ 99. There shallbe electedat the regular election
in November , one thousand eighthundred and ninety-
three, and every four years thereafter, by the quali-
fiedvotersof the city,a prosecutingattorney of the
policecourt,who shallhold his officefour years,and
untilhis successoriselectedand qualified.
§ 100. No person shall be eligibleto the officeof
prosecuting attorneyof the policecourt who ,at the
time of his election, is not an actual resident of, and
qualifiedvoter in, the city,and a licensedpracticing
lawyer for one year next before his election.
§101 . He shall enter upon the discharge of the
duties of his officeon the firstMonday in December
next after his election .
§ 102. It shallbe hisduty to attend all sessionsof
the police court, and to prosecute all criminal and
penal casesin saidcourt, whether forviolationsof the
ordinances of the cityor the laws of the State,and
any appeals from same .
§ 103. He shall receive as his compensation thirty
per cent.of allfinesand forfeituresrecoveredin said
court,and which shallbe collectedand paid intothe
treasury of the city,the same to be ascertainedfrom
the monthly settlementsof the marshal, and paid
upon order of the council.
Revenue and Taxation .
§ 104. All real and personal estatewithin the city
on the tenth day of January in the year in which the

1092 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
assessmentshallbe made , and of allcorporationshav-
ing theirchief officeor place of business in the city
on saiddate,and the franchisesof same ,shallbe sub-
ject to assessment and taxation for alllocal and mu-
nicipalpurposes, unless exempt from taxation by the
Constitution, and the same shallbe assessedat itsfair
cash value, estimatedat the price it would bring at a
fairvoluntarysale. Any franchisegranted, in whole
or in part, by a city, and exercised within said city,
may be taxed by saidcity, notwithstandingthe corpo .
rationowning or exercisingsame may have its chief
officeor place of business elsewhere .
§ 105. The cityshallhave a lien for fiveyears from
the date of assessment on the property assessed, and
on all other property, of each person or corporation,
for all taxes due by them respectivelyto the city,
which shallnot be defeatedby gift,devise,sale, alien-
ation, or any means whatever, and nothing shall be
exempt from levy and saleunder a tax warrant .
$ 106. For the purpose of taxationrealestateshall
includeland and the improvements thereon, and per-
sonal estate shall include every other species and
characterof property,that which is tangibleand that
which isintangible.
§ 107. The holder of the legal titleand the holder
of the equitabletitle, and the claimant or bailee in
possessionof property of allkinds ,on the tenth day
of January , shall be liablefor the taxes thereon, and
itmay be assessedtoeither,and shallbe valuedwith-
out regard to any conflictingtitle.
§ 108. In assessingreal estate,each lot or parcel of
ground shallbe given in a separateitem,togetherwith
the number of each on the map of the city, and the
streetand square where situated,and the number of
feetfront, and the depth ,and how much frontage on
each street where it binds on more than one street.
The value of the ground and the improvements shal ',
as to each lot, be statedseparately.
§ 109.Allpropertyshallbe assessedand valued as

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1093
of the tenthday of January of each year, and the as-
sessorshall begin his work on that day , or as soon
thereafteras practicable,and have the same completed
by the tenth day of March following,and return the
same to the common council .
§ 110. The common council shallprescribeand pro-
vide , for the use of the assessor, books and blank
scheduleswith appropriateheadings and columns .
§ 111. The provisions of the law for the assessment
of property fortaxationfor State purposes are hereby
adopted as part of this act, as far as same is appli-
cable, except as hereinotherwiseprovided.
§ 112. During the month of March of each year the
mayor shall, subject to the approval of the council,
appoint threeintelligenthousekeepers residingin dif-
ferent wards of the city, who shall constitutethe
board of tax supervisors for said city.
§ 113.The saidsupervisorsshallconvene in thecoun-
cilhallon the firstMonday in April in each year, and
proceed to the discharge of theirduties . The assessor
shall meet with them and act as their clerk . The
supervisorsshall supervise all assessments, and see
that all property is assessedat its fair cash value,
taking into considerationthe width,depth and loca-
tion of lots,and the cost and condition of improve-
ments and personalproperty.
§ 114. The provisions of the law governing the
county supervisors of tax are hereby adopted as
part of thisact as far as same is applicable, except
as otherwise herein provided.
§ 115. The supervisorsbaving finishedtheirwork ,
shall return the tax -book to the city clerk, who shall
submit same to the common council, and the council
shallhave power to make such changes in the assess-
ment or valuation of any listas may be just and
equitable; and any person aggrieved by any action
of the assessoror supervisormay appeal to the com-
mon council .
70 - L

1094 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 116. After the tax book shall have been amended
or changed as above, the common council shall, by
resolution,acceptthe tax-book, fixthe rateof taxa-
tion for the year, and place it in the hands of the
clerk to be copied, and the clerk shallwrite out the
amount of taxesdue on each listin such form as may
be prescribedby ordinance.
§ 117. The clerk shalleach year,afterthe comple-
tion of the work of the board of supervisors, make
out, as hereinafterdirected,or in such form as may
be prescribed by ordinance, tax book or receipts
againstsuch personsand property as may be assessed,
and deliver them not laterthan the firstday of June
to the tax collector.
§ 118. Before delivering the tax -book or tax -receipts
to the city collector, the clerk shall take from the
collectora receiptfor the fullor gross amount of taxes
due saidcity, which receiptshall be presented to the
common council and recorded in itsminutes , and the
collectorshall be charged with the full or gross
amount of said taxes .
$ 119. All taxes shallbe due and payable as soon as
the tax-book or tax-receiptcome to the hands of the
collector, and said tax-books or receiptsshallhave in
the hands of the collectorallthe effectand operation,
to allintentsand purposes, of an execution at law in
favorof the cityand againsteach and every tax-payer
and his property forthe payment and satisfactionof
the amount of tax againsteach tax-payer respectively.
§ 120. The collector, by virtue of said tax-book or
tax-receipt, shallhave like power and authority to
levy upon the personalproperty of the respectivetax-
payers to satisfythe amount due from each tax-payer
respectively, that sheriffsof thisCommonwealth have
to levy executionsin theirhands upon personal prop-
érty,and shallhave likepower givenby law to sheriffs
forthe collectionof taxesby garnishment .
§ 121. In the collectionof taxes placed in his hands
for collection, the collectormay distrain the goods

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1095
and chattelsowned by or in the rightfulpossessionof
the person from whom thetax isdue ,notwithstanding
the existence of any lien upon the same .
§ 122. The common council may , by ordinance,
make such discountforthe prompt payment of taxes,
and prescribesuch penaltiesfor the dilatorypayment
of taxes as,in itsdiscretion, may be proper.
§ 123. It shallbe the duty of the collectorto list
fortaxationany property he may findhas been omit-
ted,and he shall report such liststo the cityclerk,
who shall indorse same on the tax -book , and charge
the collector therewith , and the collector shall have
the same power to collectsaid listsas if they had
been originallylistedby the assessor.
§ 124. It shall be the duty of the collectorto make
out a statement twice in each month of the total
amount of money received by him as collector since
his lastreport,giving the names and amounts paid by
each tax-payer. This statement shallbe sworn to by
the collector, and , together with the totalamount of
money receivedby him , shall be immediately deliv-
ered to the treasurer of the city.
§ 125. The collectormay , by and with the consent
of the common council, appoint a deputy collector,
who shallhave the same power and authorityas the
collector,and the collectorshallbe liableon his official
bond forthe actsof such deputy .
§ 126. On the firstday of January in each year,
afterthe assessmentof the citytaxes, the tax collect-
or shall make out tax billsagainst all persons or
corporationsowing taxes on real estate, with a de-
scriptionand location of the property assessed, and
upon which taxes are due , and the amount thereof,
which billsshallbe certifiedby the cityclerkas being
true and correctlists,and he shallfurnishsaidlistsor
billsto the city attorney, whose duty it shallbe to
bring without delay suitsfor the recovery thereof in
the circuitcourt of the county .
§ 127. Said listsor bills,when certifiedby the city

1096 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
clerk,shallbe prima facie evidencethat the property
was properly listedby the cityassessor, that the ordi-
nance levyingthe taxes was duly adopted,and that all
the steps necessaryto secure a lien on the property
have been duly and properly taken,and that the per-
son owing the taxes has no personal property in the
cityout of which saidtaxes could be made .
§ 128. In these suitsthe cityshall, unless there be
cause to the contrary, ask for and obtain a personal
judgment againstthe person assessed,as wellas the
enforcement of the lien for taxes hereinbefore given .
§ 129. The action herein authorized shall, in all
respects, be conducted like suits upon liens arising
upon contracts, and saidcourtsshallhave jurisdiction
of all suits for the enforcement of the collection of
taxes as herein provided .
§ 130. The sums due for taxes for earlier and later
yearsshallbe included in the petitionand judgment .
All taxes shallbear interestat the rate of eight per
centum per annum from the firstof October of each
year. When allthe taxesdue by the same owner , or
set of jointowners, exclusiveof interest, amount to
lessthan twenty dollars, the cityattorneymay com-
bine with other bills or sets of bills, also less than
twenty dollars,having due regard to the nearness of
location,in one action, so that the aggregate of the
billsembraced in one suitmay amount to fiftydollars
or over .
§ 131. Whenever taxes due for a year preceding
the assessment of one thousand eight hundred and
ninety-three are included in an action brought pur-
suant to the next preceding section are set up by
pleading in any action, the material facts making
them a lien on the property to be subjected shall
be stated in the petitionor other pleading, and
the court shall render judgment according to the
justiceof the case; and this rule shall also prevail
in all actions which may be brought exclusively
for the taxes of such former years ; and such actions

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1097
areherebyauthorizedupon allpastdue taxeson real
estate .
§ 132.The fiscalyear shallcommence on the first
day of June .
§ 133.No erroror irregularityinthe assessmentof
property shall invalidatethe assessment or any pro-
ceeding under same . Where any property has not
heretofore been assessed for taxation , or where same
has been irregularlyor improperlyassessed,orwhere
noticeof the time and place of the meeting of the
board of supervisorsof tax has not heretoforebeen
properly or regularlygiven,saidcityis authorizedto
pass an ordinance directingthe assessment of such
property and making correctionin any assessment
irregularlyor improperlymade ,and givingnoticeof
the placeand time of the meeting of any board of tax
supervisors, so that any tax-payer may appear before
same . The objectof this sectionisto insure the col-
lectionof any unpaid taxes, and forthat purpose any
of said citiesmay ,if itdeems it necessary, appoint a
specialboard of tax supervisors,whose duty it shall
be to assessfor taxation any omitted property or to
correctany improper or irregularassessments, or to
hear any complaints as to the assessment or value of
any propertyupon which thetaxeshave not been paid.
When any property is assessed or any assessment
corrected,or where any tax-payer has the opportunity
of being heard for previous years, the tax rate and
leviesand lienfor such years shall apply, and the
provisions of this section shall not extend back for
more than fiveyears .
Collector of Taxes .
§ 134. The council shallelectat their firstregular
meeting in April,in each year, a collectorof taxes,
whose duty itshall be to well and faithfullycollect,
account for, and pay over to the city treasurer, all
taxes leviedby the city,and who shallhold his office

1098 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
for one year, and until his successorshallbe elected
and qualified.
§ 135. No one shall be eligibleto the officeof col-
lectorof taxes unlesshe be a male citizenof the city,
at leasttwenty-one years of age,and a bona fide resi-
dent of said city; but no collectorof taxes shall be
eligibleto re-appointment or re-electionto the same
office until he shall have made a settlement with the
councilof all his accounts, and paid over all money
in his hands , and have obtained from said council a
quietus .
§ 136. If thereisno personalproperty out of which
the collector can make the taxes, and the same are
not paid within four months after he receives the tax-
books forthe collection,he may levy on any realestate
in said citybelonging to or listedto said delinquent
tax payer, and sellso much thereof for cash as will
pay the taxesdue ,commissions and costs; but in this
regard the laws of thisCommonwealth governing the
powersand dutiesof sheriffsinlevyingon and selling
property under execution shall be applicableto the
collector, and regulatehis powers and actionas to the
order and manner of levying on property, the mode
and time of advertising and place of sale; and it shall
be the duty of the mayor of said cityto bid therefor
the taxes due , commissions and costs of sale; and
when said sums are bid on said property, the same
shallbe struck offto him for benefitof the city, un-
lesssome one elseoffersto pay saidamounts for a less
quantity of land.
§ 137. The owner of the realestateso sold,his heirs,
representativesor assigns,shallhave the right to re-
deem the same from the purchaser thereof, at any
time within two years from the day of sale,by pay-
ing to the purchaser the amount bid by him at said
sale, with eight per cent.per annum interestthereon
from the day of sale: Provided , If the owner of said
propertyisa minor, marriedwoman or of unsound
mind , and the same is not redeemed according to the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1099
above terms,and within the two years,by said owner
or owners,or by some one for them , the purchaser,
other than the city, may bring suit in equity in the
circuitcourt in said county to have so much of said
property so purchased sold as may be necessaryto pay
the said sum bid at collector'ssale, with interestat
ten per cent. per annum and costsof suit,and for any
other taxes appearing to be due and payable on said
propertyat the renditionof judgment in saidsuit;
and the plaintiffshall, before any judgment is ren-
dered ,give good securityto the defendant,to be ap-
proved by the court,that he will pay off any and all
taxesforwhich saidpropertyisadjudged tobe sold,
and produce to court and filein the cause proper and
legalreceiptsfor such taxes before any deed ismade
to the property sold; but no such suitagainstpersons
under disabilityshall be brought after fiveyears shall
have passed from the day of saleby the collector, and
any such suitnow pending,unlessbrought within said
time , shall abate as to said claim or claims .
§ 138. In all cases when the collectorshalllevy on
realestate, itshallbe his duty to evidencehislevy by
writing,which in each case he shallmake out upon a
separatepaper, and shallstatethereinto the follow-
ing effect: " By virtueof the collector'stax book for
the year , placedin my hands for collectionby
the council,I have thisday leviedupon the following
described real estate(here give a descriptionof the
property) as the property of A B , to satisfythe un-
paid taxes of the said A B for the , in favor of
the city,and the costsof sellingthe same ." The col-
lector,aftersellingrealestatefor taxes,shallreturn
upon the same paper upon which his levy is written
a descriptionof the real estatesold, the time and
place of sale, that he firstadvertised the same accord-
ing to law , the name of the purchaser, and the
amount paid by him for the property sold, all of
which levy and returnthe collectorshallreturn to the
officeof the clerk of the council, which shall be re

1100 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
corded or bound in a book kept for that purpose by
the clerk, and the original return of the collector shall
also be filedand preservedin his office; all sales, re-
turns and settlementsshallbe made by the collector
prior to the close of the fiscalyear .
§ 139. The collectorof taxes shallgive to the pur-
chaser of any property soldby him fortaxes a certifi-
cateof purchase, containinga descriptionof the land
purchasedby him,time of sale, and quantity sold,
the priceat which sold,the amount of taxes sold for,
and the year or years such taxes were owing ; and
such certificateshallbe sufficientlydescriptive, if set
out with such certaintythat the property can be con-
venientlyfound,from an inspectionof the map of the
city; and if the property is not properlydescribed in
saidmap , then the descriptionof the collectormust
be otherwise sufficientto enable the purchaser to find
the property. All certificatesof purchase shallbe re-
corded in a book (or bound in a book ),kept for the
purpose, by the clerkof the councilof said city as
a public record. But the collector'sreturns of any
such salesto the mayor for the use of the city shall
be deemed his certificateof such salesand purchase
by the city.
§ 140. If any realestatesoldby the collector, and
purchasedby any one,exceptthemayorofthecity,
and except that soldas the property of minors, mar-
ried women or persons of unsound mind , is not re-
deemed within the time provided herein,it shall be
the duty of the collector, or any one of his successors,
to convey by deed said land to the purchaser, em-
bodying a copy of said certificateof salein his deed .
§ 141. The person whose realestatemay be sold by
the collectorto other persons than the mayor , his
heirs or personal representatives, may , at any time
within one year from day of sale, redeem the same
by paying, or tendering,in caseacceptanceof pay-
ment isrefused,to the purchaser, or his representa-
tives, the full amount for which the property was

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1100
sold,with twenty per cent.interestper annum there-
on until paid or tendered. In case the purchaser
refused to accept payment thereon, or in case the
purchaser be a non -residentof the city,the owner may
depositthe amount and interestwith the treasurerof
said city and take his receipttherefor,and shall at
once filesuch receiptwith the clerk of the council,
which shalloperate as a redemption of the property
sold. If the property purchased shall not be re-
deemed as aforesaidwithin a year from the day of
sale,then at the end of the year the purchaser shall
be entitledto a deed for the property, and the col-
lectorwho made the sale, or his successorin office,
shall execute and acknowledge before the county
court clerk a deed of conveyance to the purchaser ,
which shallpass all the title, legalor equitable, of
the originalowner to the purchaser.
§ 142. All sales of real estate that shall be made
under and by virtue of the power and authority of
said collector, and alldeeds of conveyance made by
him in pursuance thereof,as provided in thisact,shall
be as valid to allintentsand purposes as salesand
conveyances of real estateby sheriffsunder execu-
tion ; and purchasers of real estateat salesmade by
collectorsunder the provisionsof thischarter shall,
to allintentsand purposes, be protectedto the same
extent, and as completely,in theirpurchases and title
thus acquired, as purchasers under execution sales
are protected by the laws of this Commonwealth .
§ 143. The collectorshall return to the officeof the
clerk of the council the " collector's tax -book " at the
end of his officialterm,which shall be preserved in
his officeas other public records, together with his
written report, showing the delinquents who shall
have failedto pay theirtaxes; and alsoallunfinished
levies,if any , made by said collectorunder saidtax-
book ,upon property remaining unsold, which written
levies,made in every casein which the taxes have not
been paid,shallbe recorded by the clerkor bound in

1102 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
a book kept for that purpose by him , as aforesaid;
and the collector,afterhis term of officeexpires , shall
have the power , and shallgo on and complete said
levies,by a saleof propertyleviedon,to satisfythe
taxes remaining unpaid, and he or his successor shall
consummate such salesby executing deeds to pur-
chasers in accordance with the foregoing provisions ;
and the collector shall make the additional and fur-
ther return to the council within six months after the
expiration of his officialterm , in every case of his
salesof property,which salesit is declared his duty
to make in all cases when the taxes are not paid ,
which last returns of the collector shall likewise be
recordedby said clerk at the end of the previous re-
turns, respectively, in the book to be kept by him for
that purpose .
§ 144. The collector, in all cases in which he shall
be unable to find property while he has tax-books or
tax receiptsin his hands upon which to levy to make
the taxes,or any part thereof, to return the fact, at
the end of his officialterm , and if such return of " no
property found " be true, then as to such uncollected
taxes the collector, in his settlement with the council ,
shall receivea creditfor the amount by delinquent
tax-payers. The council,before giving the collector
credit,or allowing his delinquent list, may question
him on oath as to the truth of his return , or hear evi-
dence of others concerning the same ; and unless the
council shall be satisfied that the taxes could not have
been made by the collectorby the exerciseof reason-
able diligence,the creditshallnot be allowed.
§ 145. In all cases of a failure or omission of the
collector to collect all taxes shown upon his tax-
books , or return his levies for the satisfaction there-
of , and to return in substance " no property found "
as to those who had no property, said collectorand
his suretiesshall be liableupon his officialbond to
the city for the amount of such taxes.
§ 146. After the collector shall make his return of

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1103
" no property found " as to any unpaid taxes, itshall
- be lawful forthe cityto instituteproceedingsby peti-
- tion,in any courthaving jurisdictionthereof,against
such delinquenttax-payers forthe satisfactionof such
taxes, and subject by attachment and garnishment ,
without giving bond , any choses in action or legalor
equitable rights or interestof the tax-payer in any
property, under the same rules and regulations, and
like remedies given by the CivilCode of Practiceof
Kentucky , to individualsin cases upon the return of
" no property found " upon an execution.
§ 147. The collectorshall receivea salary or per
centum , to be fixed by the council, to be paid out of
the treasury, as may be prescribedby ordinance, for
collectingand accounting for the taxes and public
dues , twenty -five per cent. of which shall be retained
until the collector shall make his final settlement and
be entitledto his quietus. No cost or commission
shall be allowed the collectoron any salesof property
which may be purchased at a tax saleby the city.
Board of Public Works .
§ 148. The common council of the cities of the third
class may ,by ordinance, establisha board of public
works , to consist of three freehold electors of said
city, and who have been bona fide residents of the
city so establishingsaidboard of publicworks for two
years next before theirappointment . Said ordinance
shall requirethe mayor of said city to appoint said
board of public works in one month from the passage
of the ordinance creating said board , and no two of
said members of the board of public works shall be
selected from the same ward . When no board of
public works has been established, the duties herein
imposed shall be performed by the common council,
and such other employes and agents as said common
council may elector designate .
§ 149. The members of saidboard shallenter upon
the discharge of the duties of their officeas soon as

1104 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
appointed, and shallhold officefour years, and until
theirsuccessorsare appointed and qualified.
§ 150. Any vacancy in said board shallbe filledfor
the unexpired term in the same way the original ap-
pointments were made .
§ 151. The compensation of the members of said
board shallbe fixedby the common council prior to
theirappointment , and shall not be changed during
their term of office .
§152. In the absence of the mayor , said board shall
have the power to designate one of their number to
act as chairman and presidingofficerpro tempore , and
he shall, while presiding,have the same power as the
mayor would have if he were present and presiding .
§ 153.Two of said members with the mayor , or
threeof them without him , shallconstitutea quorum
for the transaction of business .
§ 154. Said board shall hold at least one meeting
each month , and as much oftener as the business in-
trustedto itscare may require. Said meeting shall be
held at statedtimes,and the mayor , or any two mem-
bers of said board, may at any time call a special
meeting .
§ 155. Said board shallkeep a correctand complete
record of its proceedings, and upon the passage of
every resolutionor order of any kind the yeas and
nays shallbe calledand enteredupon the journal, and
immediately after the adjournment of each meeting
the proceedingsshallbe published in some newspaper
published in the city,to be designated by the board
at the beginning of the year .
§ 156. The auditor of the cityshall ex officiobe the
clerkof said board , and shallbe the custodian of its
records, and copies of same ,attestedby him as clerk
of saidboard,shallbe competent evidenceinallcourts.
§ 157. No person shallbe eligibleto membership in
saidboard who isrelatedto the mayor or any member
of thecouncilwithinthe thirddegreeof consanguin-
ityor affinityunder the civillaw.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1105
3 § 158. The members of the board shall have seats
in thecommon councilof the city,and be entitledto
take part in its debates, proceedings and delibera-
tions,on allquestions relatingto matters under their
charge, subjectto such rulesas the councilmay from
time to time prescribeby ordinance,but withoutthe
right to vote .
§ 159. Except as may be herein otherwise provided,
the said board shall have the exclusive right to em-
ploy the chiefs and heads of departments : firemen ,
work -house,hospital,alms-house and cityprison em-
ployes ; engineers,clerks,superintendents,scavengers,
and such other persons as itmay be deemed necessary
for the proper execution of itsduties, and remove
same at itspleasure, and fix theirsalariesand com-
pensation; but the salariesof allsuch employes ,ex-
cept common laborers, shallbe fixedwithin the limits
prescribedby the common council. The said board
shall not employ or contractwith any person who is
relatedto any member of saidboard,or to the mayor ,
or to any member of the common council within the
third degree of consanguinity or affinityunder the
civil law .
§ 160. The board of public works shallhave the ex-
clusive power and control over the construction,
supervision, cleaning, sprinkling, repairing, grading
and improving of allstreets, alleys, avenues, lanes,
public cemeteries, parks, squares, fountains, wharves
and landings, market -houses and places, bridges,
sewers,drains, ditches, culverts, streams, canals and
water-courses, side-walks, curbing, and the lighting
of allsuch publicplaces as may be deemed necessary
within the corporation; to fixand establishthe grades
of allstreets, alleys, avenues and thoroughfares; they
also have the power , supervisionand controlover the
construction,repairing,cleaning,lightingand heating
of allpublic buildings, and over all public improve-
ments of the city. They shall have the exclusive
power to make allimprovements and expenditures,

1106 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
within the revenue limits allowed by the council,
which shallcost less than fivehundred dollars, but
shall let all contracts over fiftydollars to the lowest
responsiblebidder afterdue advertisement. The said
board shallhave the exclusivepower to organize and
controlthe firedepartment ,the work -house , the hos-
pital, the alms-house,the cityprison, and such works
as the city may own for supplying the city or the
inhabitantsthereofwithwater,light,heator power .
§ 161. That the board of public works shall, on or
before the day fixed in each and every year for the
tax levy,prepare and submit to the council an item-
ized estimate of the amount of money necessary and
advisable,in their opinion, to spend in the execution
of the duties intrustedto them for the ensuing year ,
givingindetailthe plansof constructionand repairs,
with estimatesof expenditures and salaries, wages ,
et cetera,proposed by them ,with the estimated cost
of each improvement or salary, specifying for what
department required; and itshall be the duty of the
common council, in their annual levy of taxes , to
make such levy as,in theirjudgment , shallbe neces-
sary and advisable; and the amount so leviedshallbe
collected and placed to the credit of the board of
public works , and shall not be used for any other
purpose,but shall remain as a separate fund in the
hands of the treasurer .
§ 162. That when the said board shall deem it ad-
visableto make a contract for the execution of any
work , or purchase of any suppliesor material for any
matter under itscharge, it shall cause to be made a
careful estimate of the cost of such work or material .
In allcaseswhere the estimated cost of any expendi-
ture exceeds five hundred dollars, the board shall
transmit to the common council, with itsrecommen-
dations, an ordinanceauthorizingthe said expendi-
ture,with an estimateof thecost. Upon the passage
of such ordinance itshallbe the duty of the board to

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1107
advertise and let the contract to the lowest responsi-
ble bidder .
§ 163. All contracts of the board shall be made in
the name of the city,and shall be executed in behalf
of the cityby the mayor under the sealof the corpo-
ration,and attestedby the cityclerk,and filedwith
the records of the board .
§ 164. That no member of the board, or other per-
son,whether in the employ of the board or otherwise,
shallhave the power to createany liabilityon account
of the board or fund under its control,except by ex-
press authorityof the board conferredat a meeting
duly and regularlyconvened.
§ 165. If,at any time, it shallappear,in the judg-
ment of the board, that the levy made by the common
council for the current or ensuing year, for the use
of the department of public works , is insufficientfor
properlyconducting the affairsof the city,and forthe
constructingof such improvements and repairsas are
committed to said board , and consistent with the
health , comfort and convenience of the inhabitants
of the city,they shallso reportto the common coun-
cil, furnishing at the same time an estimateof such
deficiency, on receiptof which it shall be lawful for
the said council, in theirdiscretion,to make such ad-
ditionalappropriations,and proceed to make a special
levy upon the taxableproperty of the city,but not to
exceed the limitprovided by thisact.
§ 166.Any contractmade in violationof the pro-
visionsof thisact shallbe void, and no money shall
at any time be paid to any person claiming under a
contract with the board of public works until such
person shallhave firstfiledwith the board his state-
ment in writing, and under oath,disclosingthe names
of allpersons,directlyor indirectlyinterestedin the
contract, or the proceeds or the profitsthereof, de-
claring that no person other than those named are
interested,and that no person forbidden by this act
has any interestin same .

1108 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 167. When in the opinion of the board it shall
become necessaryin the prosecutionof any work to
make alterationsor modificationsin the specifications
or plans of a contract, such alterationsor modifica-
tionsshallonly be made by order of the board , and
such order shallbe of no effectuntilthe price to be
paidforsame shallhave been agreedupon in writing,
and signed by the contractor, and approved by the
board . The total cost of the work , with the addition
of the priceso agreed upon ,shallnot exceed the orig-
inal estimate .
§168 . No contractorshallbe allowed any thing for
extra work caused by an alterationor modification
unless an order is made and an agreement signed as
providedintheprecedingsection,nor shallhe in any
case be allowed more for such alteration or modifica-
tionthan the pricefixedby such agreement .
§ 169. The board of public works shall employ a
competentengineer,who shallbe styledthecityengi-
neer. He shallkeep an officein said city, and see
that allpublicworks and improvements are executed
accordingto contract, plans and specifications, and
certify to same .
$ 170. The board of publicworks shall have charge
of all expenditures for paupers,and shall employ a
competentphysicianforsame,who shallkeep an office
in thecity,and be known as thecityphysician.

ordinances to require the improvement of streetsand
alleys,subjecttothe mode and manner hereindesig-
nated,eitherby grading and paving,or by grading,

portionthereof,not lessinlengththan one block,or
the whole of any strector alley now established, or
which hereaftermay be established, or by guttering,
curbing,paving and gradingany number of feet,not

lessthan a block,upon eitheror both sides of any

or repaving on any or all streetsat the cost of the

ment ; and the cost of such improvement to be appor-
tioned accordingto the number of feetfronteach may
own in front of said improvement ; and a lien is here-
by createdon the saidground forthe costthereof; all
of which improvements , when required to be made ,

ing the side-walk ,the whole or any part, not lessthan
one block of any street.
§ 172. Upon the passage of any ordinancerequiring
the improvement of streetsor alleys, or partsthereof,
itshallbe the duty of the mayor to enterintoa con-
tractwith any person or persons offeringor agreeing
to do the work requiredto be done by such ordinance,
upon the best and most advantageous terms ; and to
enable him to do this he shall advertise the letting of
such work in some newspaper publishedin said city,
for at least ten days , for sealed proposals for the
doing of said work , and reportsuch contract,in writ-
ing , as he may be able to effectto the common council
for its acceptance or rejection. All contractsthus
made with the mayor and the person or persons pro

posing to do the work shallnot be binding until rati-
fiedby the common council; and the common council
shallrequire of such contractoror contractors bond ,
with good security,to be approved by the common
council, conditioned for the faithfulperformance of
said contract .
§ 173. The work contracted to be done shallbe per-
formed under the supervisionof the mayor and en-
gineer, and subject to the acceptanceof the common
council; and it shallbe the duty of the engineer to
make out and reportto the common council complete
and accurate estimates and apportionments of the
costof saidwork and improvements againstthe owners
of propertyliabletopay the same,designatingeach
one'srespectiveliability.
§ 174. When the common council shallhave passed
an ordinancerequiringsaid improvements to be made ,
and when a contractin pursuance to such ordinance
shall be executed and ratifiedas aforesaid, and the
common councilshall have received the report of the
engineer estimatingand apportioning the cost of the
work , and ,by order or resolution, shallhave received
said work as done according to contract, then the
liabilityof the owners of the property chargeablewith
the cost of said work shall be fixed, and there shall
be a lien on the property for same .
$ 175. The liensgiven for the purposes named in the
lastfour sectionsmay be enforcedby filinga petition
in equity in favor of the contractoror his assignee,
against any or all the persons liablefor the cost of
said work , and it shall only be necessary for the
plaintiffin the actionto filea copy of the ordinance
of the common councilrequiringthe work to be done ,
a copy of the contractfor doing the same , a copy of
the engineer'sreport showing the respectiveliability
of each person sued,a copy of the order of each reso-
lutionof the common council receivingthe work as
done according to the contract, which shall be , to-
getherwith the correspondingallegationsin thepeti-

T tion, prima facie evidence of the plaintiff'sright
of recovery, and in such cases the defendant shall
not be allowed to make the defense that the work
was not done according to contract, as against said
plaintiff in the action ; and it shall be lawful for
the court hearing the case to adjudge a sale of so
much of the lotor lotsof each person as willpay his

thereon at the rate of ten per centum per annum , to-
gether with alltaxes and leviesmade upon such prop-
erty which the purchaser may have paid or become
liable subsequent to the sale thereof ; and if not re-
deemed within the time specified, the sale shallbe

pired, the court shall cause a conveyance to be made
to the purchaser by the commissioner. The plaintiff
may have a personaljudgment againstthe defendant
liableto the cost of said work , and have execution
thereon ; but ifthe executionissue, then no saleof lot
or lotsshallbe made by the commissioner until the

tionof such sum only as shallappear to be unsatisfied
by the return on the execution .
§ 176. The cost of any improvement to the owners
of the property, ordered to be made at the cost of
owners of lots, shall,in no case,exceed one-half the
value of the lot or lotsin front of which said improve-
ments shall be made ; and for the purpose of ascer-
taining the value of lots in front of which the street
isproposed to be improved ,the common councilshall,
on motion of any owner of a lotwho allegesthat the
proposed improvements willcost more than one-half
thevalueof thelot,appointone person,and thesaid

owner shallname one person, and the two so selected
shallchoose a thirdperson,and the threepersons thus
selected,afterbeing sworn ,shallvalue the lot or lots
in frontof which the improvement is proposed to be
made , and the valuation shall be made before the im-
provement is made ,and the property shall be valued
in itsunimproved state; and if the cost shall exceed
one-half the value of the property, then the city shall
pay to the contractorsthe excessover the one -half the
value of the property.
§ 177. All the improvements of the streets ordered
to be made at the expense of the property -owners
shall,tothe extentmade ,and when paid for,exempt
saidproperty-owners from any specialliabilitythere-
after for the cost of keeping said improvements in
repair, except in casesof repairingor reconstructing
side-walks, curbs and gutters. The mayor shallhave
power and authorityto notifyownersof lotsfronting
the same to make repairsupon side-walks,in any case
where the costof such repairsof side-walks shall not
exceed ten dollars, in front of any one owner's lot ;
and in case such owner shall not, within five days
afterhe or she has noticeof such side-walk being out
of repair, repairthe same ,the mayor is authorized to
have the same done at the cost of the owner , which
repairs,ifnot paid by the owner ,shallbe paid by the
city,and shallbe a lienupon the lotforthe payment
thereof, and the cityshallhave the same remedies for
the collectionthereof, with costs of suit, that are
given in casesfor improving streetsto the contractor.
§ 178. In allcases of the improvement of the streets
and gutters, curbs and paving side-walks , the im-
provement of the intersectionsthereof shall be paid
by the city.
§ 179.The common councilshallhave power to cause
to be graded, constructed, reconstructed, paved , or
otherwise improved and repaired, all streets, side-
walks , alleysand publicways , or parts thereof,with-
in the city, of such material and in such manner and

§ 180. That on the trialin equity of any case relat-

Elections .
§ 182. All popular elections and registrations for
same shallbe held under the generalelectionand reg-
istrationlaws of the State,and as thereinprovided.
§ 183. All officers, electiveby popular vote under
this charter, shall be elected at the regular electionin
November , one thousand eight hundred and ninety-
three, and thereafteras their terms shall expire or
their officesbecome vacant .
Judicial Department - Police Judge .
§ 184. The judicialauthority, except as otherwisə
herein provided,shall be vestedin a magistrate who
shallbe styledthe policejudge.
§ 185. The police judge shall be elected by the
qualifiedvoters of the cityfor a term of four years,

1114 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and shallenterupon the dischargeof the duties of his
officeon the firstMonday in December next after his
election, and shall hold until his successor is elected
and qualified. The policejudgeelectedin November ,
one thousand eight hundred and ninety-three, shall
not enter upon the discharge of the duties of his
officeuntilthe firstday of September ,one thousand
eight hundred and ninety-four, and hold officeuntil
hissuccessoriselectedand qualifiedin one thousand
eighthundred and ninety-seven.
§ 186. No person shallbe eligibleto the officeof
policejudgewho ,at thetime of hiselection,isunder
twenty-four years of age,and who is not a qualified
voterin saidcity, and who has not been an actual res-
ident thereoftwo years next preceding his election.
§ 187. The policejudge shallbe the presidingofficer

isteroaths, issueprocess, admit to bail,take recogni-
zances, hold examining courts, preserveorder, make
arrests, and punish for contempt ,conferredby law on
justicesof the peace.
§ 188. He shallbe paid an annual salaryof six hun-
dred dollars, payable monthly out of the city treas-
ury ; but the same may be increased by ordinance,
but not during his term of office.
Police Court .
§ 189. A policecourt is hereby establishedin each
cityof the third class,to be held daily,except Sun-
days , in a suitableroom to be provided at the expense
of the city.
§ 190. Said courtshallbe a court of record, and all
record-books and blanks necessary therefor shall be

diction of all prosecutions for violations of the ordi-
nances or by -laws of the city occurring within the

corporatelimitsof the city,and the offendermay be

or arrested in the city or elsewhere . It shall also

all bailsand recognizancesentered into or deposited

the name of the Commonwealth for the benefit of the

shall,when collected,be paid into the treasuryof the

fund of the city, except thirty per cent. of same ,

§ 193. The judge of the policecourt shall be the
clerk of his own court, and shall tax as cost for his

the peace in penal cases; but his costshallbe for the
benefitof the city, and shall, when collected,be paid

of same , duly attestedby him , shall be competent

§ 194. The police court may act as an examining
court, as justicesof the peace or judge of the county
court are or may be authorized to act, and when so
actingthe presidingjudge of same shall be allowed

1116 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the same fees, to be paid in the same way , and they

§ 195. In allcases prosecuted in the police court the
defendant shallstand committed to the city prison
untilthe fineand costs are paid or replevied, not to
exceed,however , one day for each dollar of said fine
and costs,and may by ordinance be required to work
in the citywork -house or on the streetsor alleys, or
any publicwork of the city,until said fineand costs
are satisfied,at the rateof one dollar per day . Per-
sons sentenced to imprisonment may be required to
work in the same way during the period of imprison-
ment .
§ 196.The common council may fix any penalty in
their discretion for violations of ordinances and by-
laws of the city,that may be imposed without the
interventionof a grand jury and the finding of an
indictment , but in no case shall it be less than that
imposed by statuteforthe same offense.
§ 197. In allprosecutionsin the policecourt where
the punishment may be imprisonment ,or where the
finemay be over fiftydollars,the cityor the defend-
ant may demand a jury. In all such cases the jury

§ 198.In prosecutionsand proceedingsin thepolice

liableforcostsunlessthe same be collectedin money
and paid intothe citytreasury.
§ 199. All appeals from the police court sha'l be

judgment , and shall be directlyto the circuitcourt

peals are taken from the quarterlycourtsto the cir-
cuit courts, as provided by law in penal cases. The
defendant may appeal in any case where the judg-

the punishment might have been a fineof twenty -five

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1117
dollarsor over,or imprisonment . No bond shallbe

§ 200. All capiasesand executions from the police

§ 201. The boundaries of allcitiesof the third class,

§ 215. All process, except as otherwise provided

§ 219. All ordinances , regulations and resolutions in

previouschartersor amendments thereto,or ordinance

cessorsare electedand qualified.
§ 222. When the population of any city in this

a judgment assigningsuch cityto the class to which

Schools .
§ 223. There shallbe maintained a system of public

ber electionin one thousand eighthundred and ninety-
three,by the qualifiedvotersof the cityat large. The
trustees so elected shall hold their offices one -half for
two years and one-half for four years,as shall be de-
termined by lot at the firstregular meeting afterthe
election. And at the generalelection,every two years
thereafter,thereshallbe electedby the qualifiedvoters
of the city at large one trustee from each ward in the

then expire. Said trusteesshallpossessthe same qual-
ificationsas are requiredfor councilman. Said board
of educationshallcontinue,and itisherebydeclared,
a body -politicand corporate,under the name and style
of Board of Education,with perpetualsuccession,and

sue and be sued,have and use a corporate seal, the

receive, hold, lease, selland dispose of real and per-

and management of the publicschoolsof the city, and

forthe dischargeof itsduties and the government of

The meetings of saidboard shallbe held in some pub-
licplace, and a correctrecord of itsproceedings shall
be kept in a book provided for that purpose ,which
shallbe a public record.
§ 224. Said board of education shall determine the
qualificationof itsmembers . It shallhave the power
to fill, untilthe next generalelection, allvacancies in

§ 225. All property now used for publicschool pur-
poses in the city,or which may at any time be owned

surety from any or all officersfor the faithfuldis-

ury to meet the draft.

§ 241. No sectionof this chapter shall be so con-

§ 242. For the reason that citiesof the thirdclass,

CHAPTER 223 .
AN ACT to providefor the more perfectand uniform indexingof
the publicrecordsin countiesof thisCommonwealth having a pop-
ulation of seventy-fivethousand or more .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That in each and every county within this Com-
monwealth having a population of seventy-fivethou-
sand or more , and constitutingof itselfa separate
judicialdistrict,the judges of the circuitand county

1128 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
courtsshall, in the month of January , one thousand
eighthundred and ninety-five,and in the same month
every threeyearsthereafter,appoint as officialindexer
of the publicrecordsin such county,some competent
person,who shallpossessthe same qualificationsof
eligibilityas a clerk of the county court, and whose
term of officeshallbegin on the firstMonday in Feb-
ruary following his appointment and expire on the
firstMonday in February three years thereafter; sub-
ject,however,toremovalatany timeby thejudges of
the aforesaidcourts for incompetency or neglect of
duty .
§ 2. Itshallbe the duty of such indexer, subject to
order of the various courts of his county, to compile
general cross-indexes to the records of such courts
and to the records contained in the offices of the
clerksthereof, arranging alphabeticallythe surnames
of the partiesin interest,appearing upon said records,
accordingto theirinitiallettersand the arrangement
of the various vowels and consonants therein, the ac-
curacy and acceptabilityof which general indexes
shallbe passed upon respectivelyby the court order-
ing the same .
§ 3. Said indexer shall, before entering upon the
discharge of hisduties, take the constitutionaloath
of office, and execute bond ,with surety in the sum of
twenty -fivethousand dollars, to be renewed annually ,
for the faithfuland accurate performance of his duties ;
saidbond to be approved by the circuitcourt of his
county,and upon which he shallbe liableto any per-
son or personssustainingdamage or lossby reason of
errorsor omissionsmade by himself or his assistantin
such indexesas may be compiledby him under the
provisionsof thisact.
§ 4. The officialindexer appointed as provided in
sectionone of this act shall receiveas compensation
forhis servicesa salaryof three thousand dollarsper
annum , and may ,with the consent and approval of
the circuitand county courts of his county, appoint

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1129
2.
two assistants,one of whom shallreceivea salaryof
twelve hundred dollarsper annum ,and the other a
salary of nine hundred dollars per annum , all of
which salariesshallbe allowed by the fiscalcourt of
such county,and paid monthly out of the funds as
may be availableforthe payment of feesand salaries
of county officers.
§5 . The officeof indexer in counties of the class
referred to in section one of this act shall be incom-
patible with any other public office.
§ 6. The officialindexer shallbe provided with suit-
able desk -room in the office of the clerk of each of
the courts and may not at any time, unless by the
consent of the court entered upon the minutes thereof,
remove any of the records from the officein which
they belong.
§ 7. All actsor partsof actsin conflictwith thisact
are hereby repealed.
Approved June 15, 1893.

§ 3. In the execution of the work the Commission-
ersare instructedto see that due diligence,economy
and carefulnessare exercised,and a carefullyitemized
account kept and presented to the board on the com-
pletion of the work , and the secretaryof the board
shalltransmitthe same to the firstGeneral Assembly
thereafter in session .
§ 4. The Auditor is authorized to draw his warrant
on the Treasurer from time to time for such sums as
may be ordered by the Commissioners ,provided such
sums in the aggregatedo not exceed the amount pro-
vided in section one of this act .
§ 5. The leaseof the laborof the penitentiarybeing
nearly expired, and it being absolutelynecessary to
put thebuildingsthereinin the bestconditionand in
the shortesttime possiblefor re-leasingor utilizing
the labor thereof, an emergency isdeclared to exist,
and thisact shall be in effectwhen approved by the
Governor .
Approved June 15, 1893 .
CHAPTER 225 .
AN ACT fixingdefinitelythe compensationof the Governor of the
Commonwealth , and abolishingall incidentalexpenses attached to
the office.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That afterthe expiration of the term of the
present incumbent , the compensation in full of the
Governor shall be six thousand five hundred dollars
($6,500) per annum , to be paid monthly out of the
Treasury .
§ 2. The Governor shallhave the present mansion
and any furnituretherein,and premises free of rent,
but no furniture, fuel, lights,water, ice or other inci-
dentals at the expense of the State.
Approved June 15, 1893.

ARTICLE XV .
How Fee bills to be Made Out .
§ 26. The fee-billsof every officershallbe made out
at length,in figures,and in plain English, and signed
by the officerin his officialcapacity .
1. No officershall demand or receive for his services
any other or greaterfeethan isallowed by law, or any
fee for services rendered when the law has not fixed on
a compensation therefor; nor any feefor servicesnot
actually rendered .
2. Where there are more plaintiffsor defendants
than one in an action at law or in equity,and they
shallsever in theirpleadingsorotherwise, so that part
of them shall cause the clerk or other officerto render
separate services for him or them , for which the
others ought not to be liable,the fees for such services
shall be charged separately to those for whom the
service shall be rendered .
3. No officerin making out his fee-bill shall omit
the name of any personproperlychargeabletherewith,
or insertthe name of a personnot properlychargeable.
4. Fees againsta person acting in a trust capacity
shall be made out againsthim in such capacity; and
such persons shallonly be liablethereforto the ex-
tent of the trust funds in their hands liable to the
payment thereof.
5. No fee-billshallbe made out, or compensation
allowedhereafter,forany ex officioservicesrendered
or to be rendered by any officer.
§27 . If any officershalldie before making out a

1152 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
fee-bill,or collectinghis fees in any case, his deputy ,
or, if none , his personal representative, may , within
two years afterthe serviceswere rendered, make out
and sign a fee-billtherefor, and may listthe same in
due time for collectionwith the proper officer, and
the same shall be collectable and distrainable as if
issuedby the deceased officerin his lifetime.
1. Any deputy or personalrepresentativewho shall
knowingly make a wrong charge,or in any respect
issuean illegalfee-bill,shallbe subjectto the same
liabilities, and the person the billisagainst shall be
entitledto the same remedies againstsuch deputy or
personal represesentativeas are given against an
officer if issued in his lifetime .
2. If any person, against whom a fee-billis issued
under this section,shall make affidavitthat the fee
was paid to the officerin his lifetime, and produce
the affidavitto the officerhaving the fee-billfor collec-
tion, such officershall forthwith return the fee-bill
and affidavitto the person who issuedit,and the same
shallthereafterbe collectableby actiononly as other
debts .
ARTICLE XVI .
When Fee - bills Due and Distrainable .
§ 28. All fees shallbe due and payable within two
months after the services are rendered .
1. The fee forthe attendance of a witness may be
distrained for immediately after it is due , and shall
retainitsdistrainablepower for three years. From
the expirationof that period,it shallonly be collect-
able as other debts of like quality .
2. The fees of all officersshall be distrainable on
and afterthe firstof January next ensuing the rendi-
tion of the services; and whether an officershall list
the same or not, it shall retainitsdistrainableforce
forthreeyears; and from the expirationof that period
it shall only be collectableas other debts of like
quality .

§ 29. No distressshallbe made forany fee-billuntil
the same is exhibited to and a demand of payment
thereof is made from the person itis against .
§ 30. Officers' fees may be distrainedfor immedi-
ately afterthe servicesare rendered, when the officer
rendering the services shall make an affidaviton the
back of such fee-billthat the person it is against is
about to leave the State with his effects, or that he
intends and is about fraudulentlyto sellor dispose of
his effectswith the intent of hinderingand delaying
his creditors in the collection thereof .
ARTICLE XVII .
Illegal Fee -Bills.
§ 31. Every fee-bill containing one illegalcharge,
or which , in any respect,is not according to what is
requiredby law, shall be void for the whole amount
thereof .
§ 32. Any officerwho shallsplitup and divide his
servicesso as to make two charges,when the law in-
tended but one charge or fee for the whole service, or
who shallknowingly make an illegalcharge,or issue
an illegalfee-bill, or collect, or attempt to collect, his
fees twice for the same services, or shall, by any in-
direction,collect,or attempt to collect, more for his
servicesthan is allowed by law,shall be guiltyof a
misdemeanor ,and fined not lessthan fiftynor more
than two hundred dollars .
§ 33. Any officerwho shallbe guiltyof any of the
offensesspecifiedin the lastpreceding sectionshallbe
considered guiltyof a high misdemeanor ,and his con-
victionthereof shall be prima facie evidence of his
guilt in any proceeding to remove him from office.
§ 34. An officerwho issues a fee-billshall be liable
to an action at the suit of any person for a distress
made on his property by virtue of such fee-bill, if it
containsany illegalor improper item, or an item for
which the services have not been rendered , or shall

1154 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
omit the name of any of the partieslegallycharge-
able therewith,or ifit issuesfor a fee against any
person not legallychargeable with the same , or that
has been paid, or if the fee-billshallnot strictlycom-
port in every particularwith the laws in respect to
fee -bills .
1. No officerissuingsuch fee-billshall, in defense
of such action,justifyunder such feebill.
2. In such actionthe findingof the jury shallnot be
less than three times the amount of the fee-bill .
§ 35. If any person shallpay any fee-billor claim
forfees, in which he believesthere is an erroneous ,
improper, or illegalitem or items,or charge or charges
forservicesnot actuallyrendered,or that the fee-bill
does not comport with the law in every respect,he may
hand such fee-billto the circuitjudge, who presides
in the county where the person who paid the bill re-
sides,eitherin vacationor in term time ; and , there-
upon ,such judge, afterinspectingthe fee-bill,if there
be any item therein not authorizedby law, or if the
fee-billdoes not comport with the law inevery respect,
shall, at the term that he receivesthe fee-bill, or, if
in vacation , at the next term of the court holden for
that county, proceed to quash such fee-bill, and order
the officerto repay the amount of the fee-billto the
personwho had paid the same,and for the costs of
the proceeding.
1. He shall,moreover, enter up a fine against the
officerwho issuedsuch fee-billin favor of the person
aggrieved, of not lessthan one dollar nor more than
four dollarsfor each illegalitem contained therein.
The production of the fee-billby the party charged
shallbe prima facie evidenceof itspayment .
2. If the court or judge isof the opinion that there
isno improper charge or illegalitem in the feebillfor
which it ought to be quashed ,he shall restore it to
the person placing itin his hands , without costs or
expense .
3. A fierifacias may issue in behalf of the person

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1155
- aggrievedfor allthe sums and costsadjudged under
thissection,which shallbe indorsed that no suretyis
to be taken .
4. Before any judgment is entered under the pro-
visionsof thissection,the defendant in the proceed-
ing must have at least two days' notice thereofby
rule or otherwise .
§ 36. If distressbe made upon the property of any
person fora fee-bill,which has any unjust or improper
charge or item therein, or any item that is not made
out in every respectagreeablyto the requirements of
law , such person may apply to the officermaking such
distressfor an exact copy of the fee-bill, whose duty
it shall be to make out and deliver the same to such
person forthwith.
1. On the receiptof such copy , the person on whom
the distressis made may lay the same before the cir-
cuit judge who presidesin the county of his resi-
dence , and if, on the inspectionthereof, the judge
shall be of opinion that the fee-bill contains one
unjust item, or one item not made out in every re-
spectaccording to the requirements of law, he shall,
by written indorsement thereon, order the officerto
stay proceedings under the distressuntilthe matter
is determined in court .
2. Upon the receiptof such crder, the officermak-
ing the distressshallobey the same ,and restorethe
property distrainedto the owner .
3. The officershall, moreover, return the fee-bill
and copy with the judge's order thereon to the cir-
cuitcourt clerk'sofficeof his county, with the facts
of the case indorsed on the fee -bill.
4. The officer who issued the fee -bill shall be noti-
fiedas directedin the preceding section.
5. At the term of the court at which the judge
makes the order on the fee-bill, or,if made in vaca-
tion,at the next term thereof, the courtshallproceed
to act on and quash the fee bill, and render judg-
ment against the officerissuing the same for the

1156 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
amounts and in the manner stated in the next pre-
cedingsection,and forwhich executionmay issue.
§ 37. No officershall be entitledto any fee in a
proceeding fora misdemeanor unless the same is re-
covered and collectedfrom the defendant, in which
cases the fees allowed and to be taxed shall be the
same as for similar services in civil cases .
ARTICLE XVIII .
General Provisions .
§ 38.The clerkof thecircuitcourt,the clerkof the
county court, commissioners, receivers, examiners ,
and the sheriffof each county having a population
of seventy-five thousand or over, shall, after the
terms of the presentincumbents, respectively, ex-
pire, and on the firstday of each month , severally
send to the Auditor of Public Accounts a statement ,
subscribed and sworn to by each of them , showing
the amount of money receivedor collectedby or for
each of them the preceding month as fees or com-
pensationfor officialduties, and shall, with such
statement, send to the Auditor the amount so col-
lected or received .
§39.Each oftheofficersmentionedinthe preceding
section shall receive an annual salary of five thousand
dollars, and the number of deputies allowed to each
of said officers, and the compensation to be received
by each,except the chiefdeputy,and theamount ,if
any, allowed for the necessaryexpenses of the office,
shallbe regulatedand fixedby an orderenteredupon
the order-book of thecircuitcourtand county court,
and signedby a majorityofthejudgesofsaidcourts;
and a certifiedcopy of said order shall, as soon as
entered,be forwarded to the Auditor of Public Ac-
counts, as shalla copy of any subsequent changes
made therein .
§ 40.The salaryof thechiefdeputy ofeach of said
officersshallbe two thousanddollarsper year,and the

§ 41. The salary of each officer, his deputies and
expenses of office, shallbe paid monthly by the Treas-
urer of the State upon the warrant of the Auditor,
made payable to the officer. If seventy-fiveper cent.
of the amount paid into the State Treasury in any
month isnot sufficienttopay the salariesand expenses
for that month ,the deficitmay be made up out of the
amount paid in in any succeeding month ; but in no
event shallthe amount paid by the Auditor to any
officerfor salariesand expenses exceed seventy-five
per cent.of the amount paid into the Treasury each
month by such officer,during his officialterm.
§42. If any officershallfailto make and filewith
the Auditor, as herein required, the monthly state-
ment ,or shallfailto remit the amount shown by such
statement at the time required, unless the time for
filingsuch statementand paying the money isextend-
ed by the Auditor, which he may do , but not for
longerthan ten days in any month , such officershall
be fined for each offense not less than five hundred
dollars .
§ 43. If any officershallknowingly omit to report,
or failto reportor make ,as herein provided,a correct
statement of all the money received or collected the
preceding month on account of his office, or shall
knowingly make or subscribeany falsestatement con-
cerningthesame,such officershallbe guiltyof a fel-
ony,and punishedby confinementin thepenitentiary
fornot lessthan one nor more than fiveyears.
§ 44. When the term of any chief officershallex-
pire, or he shall die or resign, or be removed from
office, he or his personal representative, trustee, or
committee,as the case may be, shallat once deliverto
his successor in officeall accounts ,claims and fees due
to such officerin his officialcapacity; and it shallbe

itor, under oath,the amount collectedthereon, and,

collected .
§ 45. The Auditor shall,upon each payment , draw
his warrant on the Treasurer in favor of the person
collectingforan amount equal to twenty per cent. of
the amount so paid into the Treasury, which shall be
in fullof the compensation allowed such collector.
§ 46. If the amount paid to any officerduring his
term or incumbency shallnot have been sufficientto
pay the salariesand expenses of such officerand his
office, the Auditor shall,out of the money collected
and paid as herein provided by his successor or the
person appointed as collector, pay to such officer, or
to the person entitledto receivethe same , an amount
sufficientto supply the deficitdue for salaries and
expenses,not exceeding seventy-fiveper cent. of the
amount of fees accrued during his officialterm , and
which shall have been collectedand paid into the
Treasury .
§47. If any officeror his personal representative,
trustee, or committee,as the case may be, shall col-
lect any fees, accounts or demands due him in his
officialcapac ty after the expiration of his term , or
afterhe has resigned or died or vacated the office, or
shall fail to deliver to his successor all the fees,
claims and accounts due to him in his officialcapac

ity,he, or his personalrepresentative, committee or
trustee, as the case may be, shall be fined not less
than five hundred dollars for each offense .
§ 48. If such successor, or the person appointed

quired, the statement required, or shallfailto pay , as

or subscribeany falsestatement concerning the same ,
he shall be guilty of a felony,and punished by im-
prisonment in the State Penitentiaryfor not lessthan
one nor more than fiveyears .
§ 49. In countieshaving a populationof seventy-five
thousand or over,the jailershall, afterthe term of the
present incumbent has expired, receivean annual
salaryof fivethousand dollars; and the number of his
deputies,guards and turnkeys,and thesalariesallowed
to each,shallbe fixedby the judge of the county court,
the mayor of the citywhere the jailis located, and
the judge of the criminaldivisionof the circuitcourt ;
but the annual salaryof the chief deputy shall not
exceed eighteenhundred dollarsper annum , nor shall
the annual salaryof each of the otherdeputies,guards
or turnkeys exceed twelve hundred dollars per annum .
The order fixingthe salariesand number of deputies,
guards and turnkeys shallbe signed by a majority of
said persons, and spread upon the order-book of the
county court,and a certifiedcopy thereof, as well as
of any subsequent changes made therein,shallbe filed
when made with the Auditor of Public Accounts .
§ 50. The jailershall,on the firstday of each month ,

clusiveof salaries,and the amount of such expenses,
as well as the amount receivedthe preceding month

by such jaileras feesor compensation,or due to him
forallservicesrenderedby him inhisofficialcapacity,
and whether due or paid by the State, or city, or

ceived. If the jailershallfailto make and return to

county , city, or United States Government , as the

well as by the amount due from the State, which lat-

missioners,receivers,examinersand sheriffina county
having a population of over forty thousand and under

ary, report to the Auditor, under oath, the amount

copy of said order, as well as a copy of any changes

made therein,shallbe filedwith the Auditor by the

§ 57. Whereas , there are many good and whole-
some changes made by thisact in regard to the fees
of public officersof this Commonwealth , and the
giving immediate effecttheretowill be of great ben-
efitto the Commonwealth ; thereforean emergency is
hereby declared to exist,and thisact shalltake effect
upon the approval thereofby the Governor .
Approved June 15, 1893 .
CHAPTER 227 .
AN ACT relatingto evidence.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. The several courts of this Commonwealth shall
take judicialnoticeof allactsand resolutionsof the
General Assembly .
§ 2. All courts,tribunalsand officers,shalltake no-
tice of the officialsignature of any officerof this
State, of the United States, or of any State or Terri-
tory in the United States.
§3 . Copies of the journal of eitherHouse of the
General Assembly , printed by the State,shall be re-
ceived as evidence, for any purpose for which the
originaljournalcould be received.

1164 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 4. A copy of any record or paper, properly filed
or lodged in the clerk'sofficeof any court, or of the
Secretaryof State, Treasurer,Register or Auditor , of
the surveyor of the county, or assessor'sbooks attest-
ed by the person having the legal custody thereof,
shall, upon proof of the executionof the original,be
admitted as evidence in lieu thereof .
§ 5. A copy of any such writing as is embraced in
the preceding sectionmay be filedin a suit thereon,
and the defendant shallplead theretoas if the origi-
nal were fil-d .
§ 6. A copy from the mayor's officeof any city, or
from theofficialbooks of any town or religioussociety,
or an ordinance or by -law for the rule of such city,
town ,or society, attestedby the keeper thereof, shall
be evidencefor any purpose for which the original
could be received .
§ 7. A copy from an entry in a clerk'sor justice's
execution book , of the date of an execution that issued ,
from such office, and to whom delivered, and when
returnable,shallbe evidencein any proceeding against
the officerand his sureties,to whom itshallbe entered
as delivered .
§8 . The certificate of the Auditor of Public Ас-
counts of the fact, and of the time of the return of
any realestateas delinquent,or of the salethereof for
taxes, or forfeiturefor non -payment of taxes, or for
other canse or concerning the redemption thereof,
shall be prima facie evidence of the facts therein
stated .
§ 9. Any county court may order any of the books
or recordsin the clerk'sofficeof such court, or in the
officeof the surveyor of the county , to be rebound :
or,when obliterated, torn, or in a ruinous condition,
to be transcribedin new books ,and shallmake a rea-
sonableallowancetherefor, which shall be chargeable
to the county .
§ 10. All courtshaving jurisdictiongreaterthan the
county courtshallhave the same power over the books

and papers in theirrespectiveclerk'sofficesas iscon-

§ 13. All recordsand exemplificationsof officebooks

record or books , and the seal of his office, if there be
a seal .
§ 14. Exemplificationsfrom the books of the exec-
utivedepartment of the United States,or any papers
filedtherein,shall be evidencewhen attestedby the
Presidentor the chiefof eitherof the departments ;or
from any Stateor Territory, of like books or papers

1166 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
when attestedby the Governor or the Secretary of
State thereof, under his officialseal.
$ 15. A copy from the record-books of any court , or

of such record or register,and the attestation, certi-
fied under his officialseal, by any consul, charge
d'affaires or minister of the United States, resident
within the sovereigntywhere the record is kept , shall
be evidence in this State .
§ 16. If any person, who shallhave resided in this
State, go from and do not return to this State for
seven successiveyears, he shall be presumed to be
dead, in any case wherein his death shall come in
question, unless proof be made that he was alive
within that time .
§ 17. The unwritten or common law of any other of
the United Statesmay be proved as factsby the parol
evidenceof persons learned in that law. The printed
books of casesadjudged in the courtsof a sisterState
may alsobe admitted as evidenceof such law.
§ 18. The existence and the tenor or effectof all for-
eign laws beyond the limitsof the United States may
be proved by the parolevidence of persons learned in
those laws. But if it appear that the law in question
iscontainedin written statute,the court may reject
such parol evidence unless it be accompanied by a
copy of the statute.
§ 19.The printedlaws of theUnited States,or of
any Stateor Territorythereof, which has been or
shallbe receivedin the Secretary'sofficeof thisState,
and which shallhave been printedunder the authority
of the United States, or snch State or Territory, or a
copy thereof,when duly certifiedby the Secretary of

State for this Commonwealth , shall be admitted and

copy thereof, such copy may be filedin the office

§ 21. Any printed volume or pamphlet of laws of

§ 22. In all criminaland penal prosecutionsnow
pending or hereafterinstitutedin any of the courts
of thisCommonwealth , the defendant on trial,on his
own request, shallbe allowed to testifyin his own
behalf , but his failureto do so shall not be commented
upon ,or be allowed to createany presumption against
him .
§ 23. The defendant requestingthat he be allowed
to testify,shallnot be allowed to testifyin chief, after
any other witness has testifiedfor the defense .
§ 24. If two or more persons are jointlyindicted,
they may testifyfor each other, unless a conspiracy

1168 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ischarged in the indictmentand proven to the satis-
faction of the court .
§ 25. If a conspiracyis charged in the indictment ,
and proven to the satisfactionof the court, then each
defendant named in the indictment may testifyon his
own behalf, as above provided.
§26. In any action,prosecutionor proceeding, civil
or criminal, which is now pending , or may be com-
menced hereafter, upon a dispute as to the genuine-
nessofthe handwriting of a person,other handwritings
of such person,though not in the case for any other
purpose ,may be introduced for the purpose of com-
parisonby witnesseswith the writingindispute; and
such writings,and the testimony of witnessesrespect-
ing them, may be submittedto the court or jury as
evidenceconcerning the genuineness of the writing in
dispute Provided , That-
1. The genuineness of such writingsshallbe proved ,
to the satisfactionof the judge,by other than opinion
evidence .
2. It must be proved, to the satisfactionof the
judge, that they were writtenbefore any controversy
aroseas to the genuineness of the writingin dispute,
and that no fraud was practicedin theirselection.
3. A party proposing to introduce such writings
must give reasonable notice of his intention to the
opposite party, or his attorney, with reasonable op-
portunity to examine them before the commencement
of the trial.
4. The judge may limit the number of such writ-
ings.
5. An errorof the judge shallbe subjectto revision
and correction in the same manner as if the error had
been committed by the court.
Approved June 17, 1898.
